Ice_Log: 1240: Noted thin seasm of ice here and there on surface of water.
1310: passed a lump of floating ice.
1400: on account of increase in amount of ice and coolness of water, hauled out to East.
1540: began running into fields of broken ice. 1500: clear of ice, stood NNE.
1645: Turned and stood SSW, it not being deemed advisable to continue to northward on account of heavy ice and young ice.
1815: stopped engine for 5 minutes in ice.
8PM to Mid: last hour passing through fields of broken ice.
2140: passing through ice.
2340: stood SSW 1/2 W and steamed ahead full speed to get clear of ice. 2300: passed a floe of broken ice 0.25 miles long.
4AM to 8AM: No ice in sight at end.
0640: thick mush ice, impeding progress of vessel through it - tasked ship and stood to Sd to clear it.
0920: in log (6.3) on account of ice.
1306: Working along beach to Sd in search of Indian village - Working to bells through young ice.
1520: Young ice making fast. 1545: hauled out SWxS 1/2 S, full speed - Field ice to Nd and Wd.

Note: 1615: Hazen Bay, Cape Vancouver bearing SxE 1/2 E 1615: Stopped

Ice_Log: 6PM to 8PM: Young ice around vessel.
8PM to Mid: Ice drifting to Nd with tide.
Mid to 4AM: Small cakes of thin ice, thickly packed, floating by ship throughout watch.
4AM to 8AM: Young ice running to NWd first hour.
8AM to Noon: Drift ice passing to Nd at end of watch.
1830: ice began passing vessel + continued coming throughout watch.
Mid to 4AM: Young ice drifting with tide first part. Merid to 4PM: Sea smooth + covered with slush ice.
4AM to 8AM: fields of fresh ice passing.
0900-1000: Passed 1 cake of mushy ice between 9 and 10 & took in log while going through it.

Note: 1150: Village Cove, St. Paul Island 1150: Stopped
